mismatched_fact_examples.truth_value: During annealing at 950 °C, austenite partially transforms to ferrite, Cr7C3, and intergranular κ-carbides. mismatched_fact_examples.prediction_value: With increasing annealing temperature from 950°C to 1050°C, ferrite fraction decreases, Cr7C3 and intergranular κ-carbides dissolve, austenite recrystallizes and grain growth occurs as α ferrite restraint diminishes.
